notes: Anonymous horizontal linear dimension: 3280mm span, DIMDOT terminators + DEFPOINTS defpoints + measurement MTEXT on DIM; a dimension cache, contains no wall geometry.

rationale.evidence: Block *D295 carries the exact AutoCAD anonymous-dimension signature: 3 POINTs on DEFPOINTS (non-plotting dimension defpoints), 2 INSERTs of the DIMDOT arrowhead/tick symbol, and one MTEXT '\A1;3280' whose value equals the geometric span (3280mm) between the extension-line handles (entities 8B52..8B5A). The two long parallel extension LINEs are vertical and the dimension LINE is horizontal, all on the annotation layer DIM.
rationale.rule: A '*D' anonymous block whose members are DEFPOINTS defpoints + dimension-terminator INSERTs (DIMDOT/_ArchTick) + a '\A1;' measurement MTEXT is the cached graphics of a dimension entity, so it is a dimension cache and its extension/dimension LINEs are annotation, never physical wall edges.
rationale.counterfactual: Had these LINEs sat on an architectural wall layer (e.g. A-WALL) as offset parallel pairs WITHOUT the DEFPOINTS defpoints, terminator INSERTs and measurement MTEXT, the block would read as plan/wall geometry and both role and wall_likelihood would flip upward; the DIM/AXIS layer plus the '\A1;' value here rules that out.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection cannot reveal WHICH physical edge this dimension measures — whether the 3280mm spans a wall thickness, an opening, or an axis gap — because an anonymous dimension block caches only its own graphics, not a back-reference to the host object it annotates.

notes: Anonymous horizontal axis-grid linear dimension: 70mm span, _ArchTick terminators + DEFPOINTS defpoints + measurement MTEXT on AA-AXIS-DIM7 (small span, terminators flipped outside); a dimension cache, contains no wall geometry.

rationale.rule: A '*D' anonymous block whose members are DEFPOINTS defpoints + dimension-terminator INSERTs (DIMDOT/_ArchTick) + a '\A1;' measurement MTEXT is the cached graphics of a dimension entity, so it is a dimension cache and its extension/dimension LINEs are annotation, never physical wall edges.
rationale.counterfactual: Had these LINEs sat on an architectural wall layer (e.g. A-WALL) as offset parallel pairs WITHOUT the DEFPOINTS defpoints, terminator INSERTs and measurement MTEXT, the block would read as plan/wall geometry and both role and wall_likelihood would flip upward; the DIM/AXIS layer plus the '\A1;' value here rules that out.
rationale.uncertainty: The projection cannot reveal WHICH physical edge this dimension measures — whether the 780mm spans a wall thickness, an opening, or an axis gap — because an anonymous dimension block caches only its own graphics, not a back-reference to the host object it annotates.
